title: Document ingested into RAG without content sanitization
description: Documents are loaded into the RAG pipeline without sanitization. Malicious documents can contain embedded instructions that poison the retrieval context.
remediation: Sanitize document content before embedding. Strip potential instruction patterns. Validate document sources.

title: Embeddings stored without quality validation
description: Embeddings are stored in the vector database without quality validation. Adversarial inputs can produce embeddings that manipulate similarity search results.
remediation: Validate embedding quality before storage. Check for anomalous embedding vectors. Implement outlier detection on new embeddings.

title: Vector database without authentication or authorization
description: Vector database is accessed without authentication. Unauthorized users can read, modify, or delete embeddings.
remediation: Require authentication for all vector database access. Use API keys or tokens. Implement RBAC for read/write operations.

title: Multi-tenant RAG without namespace isolation
description: RAG queries are executed without tenant-specific filtering. In multi-tenant applications, users can retrieve documents belonging to other tenants.
remediation: Implement namespace or metadata-based tenant isolation. Filter all queries by tenant/organization ID. Audit cross-tenant access patterns.

title: RAG data source without integrity verification
description: RAG corpus data is fetched from remote sources without integrity verification. An attacker who compromises the source can poison the entire knowledge base.
remediation: Verify checksums of downloaded data. Use signed URLs or authenticated sources. Implement content integrity monitoring.
